sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Angela, presented with a complaint of kidney stones, which she has experienced multiple times in the past, reporting that this is her seventh or eighth occurrence.
She had visited the emergency room last week and believes the current stone has passed.
She denies any current pain and blood in her urine.
She was given Percocet at the hospital for pain management.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension and diabetes.
She has been taking Norvasc 2.5mg daily for hypertension and Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes.
She reports that her blood pressure has been well-controlled at home, but her blood pressure in the office was 150/90.
Review of systems: The patient denies chest pain, belly pain, and any other symptoms.
Current medications:
Norvasc 2.5mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 150/90
Physical exam: The patient's lungs are clear bilaterally, with no crackles.
The heart exam reveals a grade 2 out of 6 systolic ejection murmur.
The abdomen exam shows no tenderness to palpation or CVA tenderness.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a history of recurrent kidney stones, which appears to be passed at this time, but may benefit from further evaluation by a urologist.
She also has hypertension and diabetes, which are being managed with medications.
Problem list: Kidney stones; Hypertension; Diabetes; Insomnia
Plan:
I prescribed 10mg of melatonin as needed for insomnia.
I referred the patient to urology for further evaluation of her recurrent kidney stones.
I will have her start taking her blood sugars before meals to monitor her glucose levels.
I will also give her a consult to nutrition to help her with her diet.
Follow-up: The patient will return in three months to check on her progress.
